ASBESTOS MOTION IN DEPT. 220, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE CONTINUED FROM DEC-21-2010 TO ASBESTOS MOTION ON DEC-23-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 220. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PER C.C.P. 36(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS MARCH 7, 2011 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 220. DISCOVERY WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL THE TRIAL DATE. SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTIONS MAY BE FILED ON 15 DAYS NOTICE, WITH NO ADDITIONAL DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS FEBRUARY 24, 2011. PLAINTIFF MUST MAKE A DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS BY FEBRUARY 17, 2011. MSC WILL BE HELD ON FEBRUARY 24, 2011 AT 1PM IN ROOM 603. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING ANY OTHER SCHEDULING OR CASE MANAGEMENT ISSUES THAT ANYONE WISHES TO HAVE ADDRESSED IN THE PREFERENCE ORDER. THE DECLARATION OF DR. FOSTER IS SUFFICIENT TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF 36(D). A CASE MANAGMENT CONFERENCE IS SCHEDULED FOR 1/13/11 AT 1:30PM IN DEPT. 220. DESIGNATED DEFENSE COUNSEL FOR BERRY AND BERRY WILL SUBMIT A PROPOSED AGENDA TO PLAINTIFF BY NOON ON 1/7/11 TO BE SERVED ELECTRONICALLY AND ANY OPPOSITION WILL BE SERVED BY CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON 1/11/11. THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANTS J-M MANUFACTURING CO. AND FORMOSA PLASTICS CORP.'S MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT HEARING OF 2/16/11 BE ADVANCED TO 2/2/11 AT 9:30AM WITH THE OPPOSITION FILED BY 1/19/11 AND REPLY DUE BY 1/26/11. DEFENDANT WESTERN NEVADA SUPPLY MAY FILE A MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE ON 15 DAYS NOTICE IF THERE IS SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER AND CIRCULATE TO ALL COUNSEL. JUDGE: JEFFREY S. ROSS; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. ***AT 2:30 P.M. IN OPEN COURT THE COURT RECUSED HIMSELF AND VACATED HIS PREVIOUS RULING. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 23, 2010 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 220. MR. NEVIN IS TO SEND NOTICE TO ALL PARTIES REGARDING THE CONTINUANCE. JUDGE: JEFFREY S. ROSS, REPORTER: LINDA FORSTER, CSR #13286 ***

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Jul-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings reported by Eric Throne, CSR #7855. Email:eric.throne.csr@gmail.com.

Geoff Sloniker, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP appeared for plaintiffs and Wrongful Death Heirs Julie Diaz and Jon Wilkins, who were not present, and with Wrongful Death Heir Gennaro DiMatteo.
Messrs. Sloniker and DiMatteo appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds negotiated after the December 18, 2014 death of William Lee Wilkins in the following proportions:

Estate of William Lee Wilkins, Deceased - ___%

Gennaro DiMatteo, as Wrongful Death Heir - ___%

Julie Diaz, as Wrongful Death Heir - ___%

Jon Wilkins, as Wrongful Death Heir - ___%

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the December 18, 2014 death of William Lee Wilkins shall be distributed in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of plaintiff Gennaro DiMatteo shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate and wrongful death claims with defendants in all claims with settlements negotiated after William Lee Wilkins' death.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds and submit same to the Court for reveiw and signature.
